description: This figure characterizes the initial microstructure and TiB₂ particle size distribution of the hypereutectic TiB₂ reinforced low-density high-modulus steel (Fe-16Mn-3.9Al-5.11Ti-2.09B wt.%, processed by vacuum melting, homogenization and hot rolling). Subfigure (a) is a pseudo 3D SEM image showing primary and eutectic TiB₂ particles on surfaces along three directions; (b) and (c) are high-magnification SEM images presenting TiB₂ morphology and distinguishing Mn-rich austenite and Al-rich δ-ferrite regions, respectively. (d) is XRD pattern confirming three phases of δ-ferrite, austenite and TiB₂. (e) shows micro-CT measured spatial distribution of TiB₂ particles in different size domains, while (f) and (g) are statistical histograms of particle number and volume fractions separately: 0.8-5 μm particles occupy the largest number fraction (~46%), while 10-20 μm and 20-42.7 μm particles account for ~56% and ~32% of total volume fraction respectively, revealing coarse TiB₂ particles dominate the total volume of reinforcements and thus dictate the bulk mechanical performance of the steel.
